title: Unit Impulse as a Generalized Function
summary: This page discusses the mathematical rigor issues with the classical definition of the unit impulse function and introduces the generalized function approach, where the impulse is defined by its sampling property rather than its pointwise values. It demonstrates that the derivative of the unit step function u(t) exists in the generalized sense and equals the impulse function δ(t), proven via integration by parts using a test function φ(t).
